message:
feat(abi,membrane): add dlfcn ABI stubs and expand runtime math modules

ABI (dlfcn_abi.rs):
- Add dladdr() ABI stub returning ENOSYS, completing the dlfcn
  family alongside existing dlopen/dlsym/dlclose/dlerror stubs.
  This enables programs that probe dladdr() availability to get
  a clean "not supported" rather than a link error.

Runtime math (membrane):
- cohomology.rs: Add Betti number computation for persistence
  diagrams, enabling topological feature extraction from the
  runtime's decision landscape
- design.rs: Add design matrix builder with condition-number
  checks for structured regression in the adaptive controller
- fusion.rs: Add sensor fusion kernel with Kalman-style update
  for combining heterogeneous runtime metric streams into
  unified confidence estimates

Also syncs beads issue tracker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-5vr.3","title":"Math: Statistical kernels - conformal, e-process, CVaR, BOCPD, alpha-investing","description":"Implement statistical decision kernels: risk.rs (conformal risk bounds, Foster & Stine 2008), eprocess.rs (anytime-valid sequential testing, Vovk/Ramdas), cvar.rs (CVaR tail-risk guard), alpha_investing.rs (FDR control), large_deviations.rs (Cramer rare-event), spectral_monitor.rs (Marchenko-Pastur/Tracy-Widom phase transitions).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldFox","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.293510913Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T07:30:55.322385957Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlibc","math","statistical"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.3","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1038,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.3","author":"WhiteRiver","text":"Progress update (WhiteRiver): fixed two cross-family coverage bugs in statistical kernels and added regression tests.

Code changes:
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/cvar.rs
  - Expanded family_from_index mapping to cover all ApiFamily variants (0..19) instead of collapsing 8..19 into Loader.
  - Added test  validating high-index family alarm accounting and max aggregation.
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/large_deviations.rs
  - Replaced hard-coded  with  so all runtime API families are tracked.
  - Added test  validating Poll (index 19) leaves calibration and reports non-empty summary fields.

Validation (all cargo commands offloaded via rch):
- rch doctor (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo fmt --check (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane high_index_ -- --nocapture (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::cvar::tests -- --nocapture (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane large_deviations::tests -- --nocapture (PASS)

This closes a correctness gap where high-index families (e.g., Poll/Process/Socket) could be undercounted or ignored by runtime tail/rate monitors.","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:47:39Z"},{"id":1039,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.3","author":"WhiteRiver","text":"Correction to prior note (shell stripped inline code formatting):

Code changes landed:
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/cvar.rs`
  - Expanded `family_from_index` to cover every `ApiFamily` variant (`0..19`) instead of collapsing indices `8..19` into `Loader`.
  - Added regression test `high_index_families_are_counted_independently` to validate high-index family alarm counting and max aggregation.
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/large_deviations.rs`
  - Replaced hard-coded `FAMILIES = 8` with `ApiFamily::COUNT`.
  - Added regression test `high_index_api_family_is_tracked` to validate `ApiFamily::Poll` (index 19) exits calibration and returns populated summary metrics.

Validation (all cargo commands run through `rch exec -- ...`):
- `rch doctor` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane high_index_ -- --nocapture` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::cvar::tests -- --nocapture` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane large_deviations::tests -- --nocapture` (PASS)

Impact: fixes a correctness gap where high-index families (`Poll`, `Process`, `Socket`, etc.) could be undercounted or ignored by runtime tail/rate monitors.
","created_at":"2026-02-15T21:47:47Z"},{"id":1040,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.3","author":"WhiteRiver","text":"Second progress update (WhiteRiver): hardened deterministic regression coverage for statistical kernel alpha-investing.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/alpha_investing.rs`
  - Replaced pseudo-golden logic in `deterministic_regression_golden` (which previously compared runtime values to themselves) with pinned deterministic baseline constants:
    - `GOLDEN_WEALTH_MILLI = 109`
    - `GOLDEN_TESTS = 825`
    - `GOLDEN_REJECTIONS = 411`
    - `GOLDEN_SUPPRESSIONS = 414`
  - Added strict drift assertions for wealth/tests/rejections/suppressions and empirical FDR.

Validation evidence (all CPU-intensive cargo commands via `rch`):
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::alpha_investing::tests::deterministic_regression_golden -- --nocapture` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::alpha_investing::tests -- --nocapture` (PASS, 19 tests)
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (PASS)

Formatting note:
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` fails at workspace scope due unrelated pre-existing drift in concurrently modified files outside this bead scope.
- Scoped check on touched crate path passed: `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check -p frankenlibc-membrane`.

Combined with the prior cvar/large_deviations fixes in this session, `bd-5vr.3` now has materially stronger correctness guards for high-index API-family handling and deterministic statistical-kernel regression detection.
","created_at":"2026-02-15T22:12:29Z"},{"id":1041,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.3","author":"WhiteRiver","text":"Third progress update (WhiteRiver): extended high-index family regression coverage to additional statistical kernels.

Code changes:
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/risk.rs`
  - Added `high_index_family_isolated_from_loader` test to verify `ApiFamily::Poll` accounting remains isolated from `ApiFamily::Loader`.
- `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/eprocess.rs`
  - Added `high_index_family_contributes_to_alarm_count_and_max` test to verify high-index families participate in `alarmed_family_count()` and `max_e_value()` aggregation.

Validation (via `rch`):
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::risk::tests::high_index_family_isolated_from_loader -- --nocapture` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::eprocess::tests::high_index_family_contributes_to_alarm_count_and_max -- --nocapture` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` (PASS)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (PASS)

Context: this complements earlier fixes in this session for `cvar` and `large_deviations`, and the pinned deterministic golden in `alpha_investing`, further reducing risk of silent high-index family coverage drift across statistical runtime kernels.
","created_at":"2026-02-15T22:13:59Z"}]}

Code changes:
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/cohomology.rs
  - Added shard wrap-around + repeated fault accumulation test.
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/persistence.rs
  - Added full-baseline-window calibration test.
  - Added abrupt topology-shift anomaly counter/state test.
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/grobner_normalizer.rs
  - Added targeted two-constraint MinorInconsistency boundary test.
  - Added fault_count transition-only increment test.
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/grothendieck_glue.rs
  - Added descent_failure_count transition-entry test.
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/clifford.rs
  - Extended summary_coherent test to assert zero drift/overlap counters in stable path.

Verification (all via rch unless noted):
- rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets (FAIL: pre-existing unrelated failure in fragmentation_storms_suite_emits_metrics)
- Focused new/changed tests (PASS):
  - cohomology::tests::shard_wraparound_and_repeated_faults_are_counted
  - persistence::tests::calibration_requires_full_baseline_windows
  - persistence::tests::abrupt_topology_shift_increments_anomaly_counter
  - grobner_normalizer::tests::targeted_two_constraint_breaks_yield_minor_inconsistency
  - grobner_normalizer::tests::fault_count_only_increments_on_structural_fault_entry
  - grothendieck_glue::tests::descent_failure_counter_tracks_entries_not_duration
  - clifford::tests::summary_coherent
- rustfmt --check on touched files (PASS, local non-CPU-intensive)

Bead remains in_progress pending broader closure criteria beyond this regression-coverage slice.","created_at":"2026-02-16T00:32:50Z"},{"id":1043,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.4","author":"CobaltForge","text":"Progress update (CobaltForge): hardened algebraic/topological kernel input hygiene + regression coverage.

  - Added tests:

    - adversarial_input_stream_keeps_summary_finite
- crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/persistence.rs
  - Added observation sanitization (non-finite -> 0.0, clamp to +/-1_000_000.0) before window ingestion.
  - Added tests:
    - non_finite_observations_are_sanitized_before_storage

- rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math::ktheory::tests::adversarial_input_stream_keeps_summary_finite -- --nocapture (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets (PASS)
- rch exec -- cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings (PASS)

Workspace-wide gates currently fail due unrelated pre-existing issues outside this bead scope:
- cargo check/clippy workspace failures in crates/frankenlibc-abi/src/unistd_abi.rs (E0503 + unused assignment).
- cargo fmt --check reports existing diffs in multiple non-owned files.

Status: keeping bd-5vr.4 in_progress; this slice closes numerical-stability hygiene gaps for ktheory+persistence and adds deterministic regression tests.","created_at":"2026-02-26T03:09:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-5vr.5","title":"Math: Control/optimization kernels - bandit, primal-dual, mean-field, Schrodinger bridge, rough-path","description":"Implement control/optimization kernels: bandit.rs (constrained MAB Fast/Full routing with regret bounds), control.rs (primal-dual threshold tuning), mean_field_game.rs (Lasry-Lions Nash equilibrium), schrodinger_bridge.rs (entropic OT between workload regimes, Cuturi 2013), rough_path.rs (Lyons rough-path signatures for syscall/memory traces).

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkBarn","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.494190448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-27T04:56:00.883316979Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["control","frankenlibc","math","optimization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.5","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1240,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.5","author":"PinkBarn","text":"Progress update (2026-02-27):
- Claimed bead and reserved files for bandit/control/mean_field_game/schrodinger_bridge/rough_path.
- Implemented robustness hardening:
  1) bandit.observe now clamps extreme u64 cost inputs before i64 math and uses saturating_sub chain for utility to prevent wraparound/overflow behavior.
  2) control.observe now uses saturating atomic counter updates (observed_calls, total_cost_ns, adverse_events) to avoid long-run wraparound.
- Added regression tests:
  - runtime_math::bandit::tests::extreme_cost_does_not_wrap_to_positive_utility
  - runtime_math::control::tests::observe_saturates_internal_counters
- Offloaded verification via rch:
  - PASS: cargo check --workspace --all-targets
  - PASS: cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
  - PASS: targeted new tests above (exact filters)
  - FAIL (pre-existing workspace state): cargo fmt --check reports unrelated formatting drift in runtime_policy.rs + runtime_math/mod.rs
  - FAIL (pre-existing): cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane --lib has 5 existing failing tests in evidence/ktheory/localization_chooser/matrix_concentration/operator_norm unrelated to touched files.
","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:44:59Z"},{"id":1241,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.5","author":"PinkBarn","text":"Progress update (continuation):
- Extended saturation hardening to additional bd-5vr.5 controllers:
  - mean_field_game.rs: saturating increments for contention/window/baseline_windows/congestion_count.
  - schrodinger_bridge.rs: saturating increments for action/window/baseline_windows/transition_count.
- Added additional regression tests:
  - mean_field_game::tests::congestion_counter_saturates_without_wrap
  - schrodinger_bridge::tests::transition_counter_saturates_without_wrap
- Offloaded verification via rch:
  - PASS targeted exact tests for all 4 new regressions (bandit/control/mean_field_game/schrodinger_bridge)
  - PASS workspace cargo check + clippy (warnings denied).","created_at":"2026-02-27T04:56:00Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-5vr.7","title":"Math: E2E tests - branch-diversity enforcement, full snapshot capture, multi-kernel interaction","description":"E2E tests: verify branch-diversity constraint (>=3 families per milestone, no family >40%, >=1 from {conformal,topology,algebra,Grothendieck}). Full RuntimeKernelSnapshot capture and replay. Multi-kernel interaction tests: verify kernels compose correctly under concurrent evaluation. Regression tests with golden snapshots.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.690977019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T23:07:11.611455979Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-test","frankenlibc","math","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.7","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.7","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-5vr.8","title":"Math: Logging - kernel evaluation traces, calibration events, drift alerts","description":"Logging spec for math kernels: TRACE for individual kernel evaluations with inputs/outputs, DEBUG for calibration adjustments and cache states, INFO for certificate loading and snapshot capture events, WARN for drift detection and regret bound exceedance, ERROR for certificate verification failures. All records include trace_id+decision_id.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.

### 2026-02-14 Progress (PeachCedar)
Implemented a focused logging slice in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs`:
- added policy certificate load-state tracking (`none|loaded|verify_failed`) in `RuntimeMathKernel`
- extended `load_policy_table` to persist successful and failed verification state
- added `export_runtime_math_log_jsonl(mode, bead_id, run_id)` emitting deterministic JSONL rows for:
  - `runtime_decision` (trace/info/warn/error mapped from action, includes `trace_id` + `decision_id` + `decision_action` + `risk_inputs`)
  - `runtime_calibration` (debug)
  - `runtime_snapshot` (info)
  - `runtime_certificate_loaded` / `runtime_certificate_verification_failed`
  - `runtime_regret_alert` and `runtime_drift_alert`
- added helper mappers (`action_name`, `decision_name`, `family_name`, `mode_name`) and deterministic trace/timestamp helpers
- added unit tests:
  - `runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_contains_required_runtime_decision_fields`
  - `runtime_math_log_jsonl_export_reports_certificate_load_outcomes`

### Verification Commands and Outcomes
- `rch exec -- cargo fmt --check` ✅ pass
- `rch exec -- cargo check --workspace --all-targets` ✅ pass (existing workspace warnings outside changed file)
- `rch exec -- cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` ❌ fails on pre-existing clippy violations in untouched files:
  - `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/evidence.rs` (`clippy::collapsible-if`)
  - `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/pressure_sensor.rs` (`clippy::manual-range-contains`)
- `rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export -- --nocapture` ✅ pass (2 tests)
- `rch exec -- cargo test --workspace --all-targets` ❌ fails on pre-existing tests in untouched file:
  - `crates/frankenlibc-abi/tests/pthread_mutex_core_test.rs`
  - failures: `futex_mutex_contention_increments_wait_and_wake_counters`, `futex_mutex_unlock_without_lock_is_eperm`

### Artifacts / References
- code diff: `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs`
- new test names listed above (same file)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.789403918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:13:36.267301078Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:13:36.267213263Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlibc","logging","math","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5vr.8","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:32Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1045,"issue_id":"bd-5vr.8","author":"PeachCedar","text":"Second-pass schema tightening completed in crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs.

 {"id":"bd-66s","title":"Stub wave 2: getaddrinfo/getnameinfo numeric+files backend","description":"Critique mapping: #3 + #4.

Deliverables:
- Implement numeric host/service resolution and /etc/hosts + /etc/services files backend.
- Deterministic error mapping for unsupported address families/protocols.

Acceptance:
- netdb fixtures pass for numeric and files-backed paths.
- Support matrix marks partial scope explicitly (DNS network backend tracked separately).

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:09.921326511Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","resolver","stubs"],"comments":[{"id":1048,"issue_id":"bd-66s","author":"CrimsonCove","text":"## Deliverables Complete

### 1. Core resolver module: crates/glibc-rs-core/src/resolv/mod.rs
- AddrInfo struct, EAI_* error codes, AF_* constants
- /etc/hosts parsing: parse_hosts_line, lookup_hosts, reverse_lookup_hosts
- /etc/services parsing: parse_services_line, lookup_service
- getaddrinfo(): numeric IPv4/IPv6 resolution, wildcard fallback
- getnameinfo(): sockaddr_in/sockaddr_in6 to string conversion
- eq_ignore_ascii_case helper
- 35 unit tests covering all functions

### 2. ABI layer: crates/glibc-rs-abi/src/resolv_abi.rs (pre-existing, 519 lines)
- getaddrinfo: numeric IPv4/IPv6 parsing, port parsing, membrane validation
- freeaddrinfo: linked list cleanup
- getnameinfo: numeric address-to-string conversion
- gai_strerror: full EAI_* error mapping

### 3. Status updated: support_matrix.json
- getaddrinfo, getnameinfo, freeaddrinfo, gai_strerror: Stub -> Implemented
- Total: Implemented 87->91, Stub 6->2

### 4. Cascading spec updates
- packaging_spec.json: symbol_distribution, Stub_remaining, blockers
- replacement_levels.json: assessment counts, stub_breakdown
- stub_priority_ranking.json: removed resolv_abi from module/symbol rankings, updated burn_down

### Test commands
```
cargo test -p glibc-rs-core --lib resolv  # 35 pass
cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness            # 213+ pass, 0 failures
```

All quality gates pass: clippy clean, fmt clean, full harness green.","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:07:39.723409Z"}]}
